mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the persistent near-zero policy rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows on an ongoing basis, producing a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of short-term fluctuations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 as of t-1mo, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or forward returns during ZIRP periods are not significantly different from non-ZIRP periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE remains above a 9% threshold for multiple consecutive months alongside stagnant PAYEMS growth, equity markets price in elevated recession risk and produce negative short-horizon forward returns as investors discount weak labor market signals.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.1% as of t-1mo, elevated post-crisis level
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following months with UNRATE > 9.0% are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0, t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year evaluation window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Depressed housing starts (HOUST) combined with near-zero federal funds rate create a joint signal: low-rate stimulus fails to revive construction activity, indicating deep structural demand weakness that predicts subdued S&P 500 returns over the next quarter until housing responds to low rates.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=571k at t-2mo, near historic lows despite FEDFUNDS=0.08
regime_hint: balance-sheet recession with ineffective rate transmission
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive on average during periods where HOUST < 600k and FEDFUNDS < 0.25% simultaneously, or Rank IC of the joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal is indistinguishable from either single-series signal (interaction term contributes < 0.01 incremental IC).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO signals resuming industrial capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which forward-predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as earnings expectations for cyclical firms improve.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=94.0419 at t-2mo showing gradual recovery from 2009 trough
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or sign accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects resilient consumer spending which comprises the majority of GDP, and this always-on momentum signal predicts positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns as consumption strength supports corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return sign accuracy conditional on positive RSAFS month-over-month growth is not above 50% over a rolling 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a 3% threshold while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets begin pricing in future policy tightening risk, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the easing regime nears its limits.
regime_hint: inflation overshoot amid zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following CPI-threshold triggers are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over available evaluation windows, or trigger fires fewer than 5 times making the test statistically inconclusive without directional confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE indicates a genuine labor market inflection rather than noise, and this combined signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following month because it foreshadows weakening consumer income and corporate demand.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS decline + UNRATE rise signal does not outperform either single-series signal in Rank IC by at least 0.02, or 20-day forward returns following joint-negative signals are not significantly more negative than returns following single-series-only negative signals.

mechanism_narrative: In a fragile post-crisis expansion regime, quarterly GDPC1 growth below trend signals persistent output gap and weak aggregate demand, predicting subdued S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings growth expectations remain capped by slack economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13271.8 billion at t-6mo, below pre-crisis trend growth path
regime_hint: fragile post-crisis expansion with output gap
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following below-trend GDPC1 growth prints are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) across at least two subsequent quarters, or Rank IC < 0.03 over the evaluation period.
